sourceRefs.fallbackText: But when people hear the word concurrency they often think of parallelism , a related but quite distinct concept. In programming, concurrency is the composition of independently executing processes, while parallelism is the simultaneous execution of (possibly related) computations. sourceRefs.fallbackText: Andrew Gerrand 16 January 2013
sourceRefs.textQuote: Andrew Gerrand 16 January 2013

sourceRefs.fallbackText: If there’s one thing most people know about Go, is that it is designed for concurrency. No introduction to Go is complete without a demonstration of its goroutines and channels.
sourceRefs.textQuote: If there’s one thing most people know about Go, is that it is designed for concurrency. No introduction to Go is complete without a demonstration of its goroutines and channels.

highSignalBlocks.text: If there’s one thing most people know about Go, is that it is designed for concurrency. No introduction to Go is complete without a demonstration of its goroutines and channels.

sourceRefs.fallbackText: To clear up this conflation, Rob Pike gave a talk at Heroku ’s Waza conference entitled Concurrency is not parallelism , and a video recording of the talk was released a few months ago.
sourceRefs.textQuote: To clear up this conflation, Rob Pike gave a talk at Heroku ’s Waza conference entitled Concurrency is not parallelism , and a video recording of the talk was released a few months ago.
